name: healthcare_emergency_exemption_proposed_redwood_auto_ben
premise: A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. Bed capacity, consent, and chart documentation are all under real-time scrutiny. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: healthcare emergency exemption proposed redwood auto ben. The moral tension is explicitly framed as autonomy versus beneficence: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. An automated failover or batch commit fires in twenty-two minutes unless a named approver intervenes. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Marco Silva
agents.identity: I am Marco Silva, the accountable decision-maker on this Healthcare bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes beneficence whenever someone invokes autonomy.

agents.name: Kaito Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Kaito Ishikawa, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so beneficence is not sacrificed by default just because autonomy feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect beneficence.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next automated step is minutes away. With partial evidence, Marco Silva must decide under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Marco Silva do?
